pattern: Optimal play on a suffix depends only on the starting index and the current cap M, so memoizing the best score over (index, M) resolves the minimax with overlapping subproblems.
hints: Once it's a player's turn, the past is irrelevant: the state is just where the remaining piles start and the current value of M.
On a turn you pick X piles (1..2M); you gain the suffix total minus whatever the opponent then optimally takes from the rest.
Memoize best(i, M) = max over X of (sum of piles from i to end) - best(i+X, max(M, X)); precompute suffix sums for speed.

edge_inputs.description: one huge pile at the end

stress.note: length capped so the plain minimax oracle stays fast
